faq_articles.title: What Should I Wear to Line Dancing Class?
faq_articles.body:
Comfortable clothing you can move in is best for line dancing. Most students wear jeans or athletic pants with a fitted top or t‑shirt. Avoid anything too tight or restrictive around your hips, knees, and shoulders.

For footwear, choose closed‑toe shoes with a low heel and smooth, non‑sticky soles so you can pivot safely. Western boots, dance sneakers, or comfortable street shoes with minimal tread all work well. Avoid high heels, flip‑flops, sandals, or heavy boots with thick rubber soles.

Layers are helpful because you may warm up quickly once class starts. Bring a water bottle and, if you tend to sweat, a small towel. There is no strict dress code, but please ensure clothing is secure and respectful, and that shoes are clean and dry before stepping onto the dance floor.

faq_articles.title: Studio Policies and Class Guidelines
faq_articles.body:
Our studio policies are designed to keep everyone safe and make classes enjoyable for all.

ARRIVALS & LATE ENTRY
• Please arrive 10–15 minutes before class to check in.
• Students arriving more than 15 minutes late may be asked to observe rather than participate, for safety.

CANCELLATIONS & NO‑SHOWS
• Class reservations may be canceled online up to 6 hours before start time.
• Late cancellations or no‑shows may result in a loss of the class credit or a fee, depending on your membership.

YOUTH PARTICIPATION
• Youth classes are separated by age range; please enroll your child in an appropriate level.
• A parent or guardian must sign a waiver for students under 18.

HEALTH & SAFETY
• Please do not attend if you are feeling unwell.
• Clean, closed‑toe shoes are required on the dance floor.

For a full list of policies, including workshops, memberships, and events, please review the detailed sections on this page or contact us with questions.

faq_articles.title: Privacy Policy and Terms of Use
faq_articles.body:
This page explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal information, as well as the terms that govern your use of our website and services.

PRIVACY
• We collect only the data needed to provide class bookings, memberships, and customer support.
• Payment details are processed securely by PCI‑compliant third‑party providers; we do not store full card numbers.
• You may request access to or deletion of your data subject to legal and accounting requirements.

TERMS OF USE
• By using our site or attending classes, you agree to our waiver and participation terms.
• Class schedules and prices are subject to change; we will do our best to communicate changes in advance.
• Misuse of the site, including fraudulent bookings, may result in account suspension.

For full legal text, including cookies and dispute resolution, please read the extended policy sections below.

faq_articles.title: How Membership Billing and Auto-Renewal Work
faq_articles.body:
Monthly memberships are billed on a recurring basis using the payment method you provide at checkout. Your first charge occurs on the purchase date, and future charges occur on the same date each month.

MONTH‑TO‑MONTH PLANS
• Month‑to‑month memberships can be canceled at any time with no long‑term contract.
• To avoid being billed for the next period, submit your cancellation request at least 3 days before your renewal date.

TERM CONTRACT PLANS
• Term contracts (for example, 12‑month plans) require commitment for the full term.
• Early cancellation fees may apply according to your agreement.

If you have questions about a specific plan, please contact us before purchase.

faq_articles.title: Youth Line Dancing Classes: Ages and Levels
faq_articles.body:
We offer youth line dancing programs for different age groups, including classes specifically designed for pre‑teens and teens.

AGE RANGES
• Ages 7–10: Kids Beginner
• Ages 11–13: Pre‑Teen Beginner & Pre‑Teen Intermediate
• Ages 14–17: Teen Beginner, Teen Intermediate, and Teen Performance

All youth classes focus on fundamentals, coordination, and confidence in a supportive environment. Please check individual class descriptions for recommended experience level and any prerequisites.

instructors.name: Emma Lee
instructors.bio: Emma specializes in high‑energy beginner and intermediate line dancing, with a focus on building confidence on the floor. She has taught across the West Coast and choreographs for local country nights.

instructors.name: Jake Martinez
instructors.bio: Jake brings a honky‑tonk vibe to the studio with classic and modern country line dances. His classes are upbeat and welcoming, especially for first‑time dancers.

instructors.name: Sophia Chen
instructors.bio: Sophia mixes traditional line dancing with pop and K‑country influences. She loves breaking down tricky patterns into simple steps.

instructors.name: Mason Clark
instructors.bio: Mason teaches intermediate and advanced combos, perfect for dancers who already know the basics and want to level up their style and timing.

instructors.name: Olivia Nguyen
instructors.bio: Olivia leads our kids and teens program, focusing on safe technique, musicality, and teamwork in a fun atmosphere.

instructors.name: Liam Patel
instructors.bio: Liam loves introducing absolute beginners to line dancing. His Saturday morning classes are a favorite for new students.

instructors.name: Isabella Ross
instructors.bio: Isabella focuses on musicality, styling, and technique, helping dancers look and feel confident at social country nights.

instructors.name: Noah Johnson
instructors.bio: Noah teaches youth and family classes, creating choreography that kids, teens, and parents can enjoy together.

instructors.name: Ava Garcia
instructors.bio: Ava’s classes blend classic line dance patterns with Latin and pop music for a fun twist on traditional steps.

instructors.name: Elijah Brown
instructors.bio: Elijah leads our popular ‘Country Night’ workshop series, focusing on social floor etiquette and multi‑level patterns.

instructors.name: Mia Davis
instructors.bio: Mia enjoys teaching small‑group beginner series where students can ask questions and progress together week by week.

instructors.name: William Kim
instructors.bio: William’s intermediate classes emphasize timing, turns, and transitions to prepare dancers for crowded social floors.
